title: Psychiatric Crisis Safe Room Allocation
description: An acute-care operations workflow that coordinates urgent intake, prioritization, and constrained resource assignment under clinical safety rules. Focus case: psychiatric crisis safe room allocation.
assumptions: Safe rooms are single-occupancy and require explicit allocation before admission.
Initial facts encode current availability of staff, rooms, equipment, and teams; availability is consumed when assigned.
Actions represent atomic operational steps (no concurrent partial effects).
Clinical authorization (psychiatric_evaluator) is required before final placement.
Teams and equipment referenced are credentialed and available when their availability predicate is true.
risks: Resource contention: limited staff, rooms, or equipment can delay or prevent placement.
Mis-triage or incorrect assignment of assessor may lead to unsafe placement decisions.
Communication failures between bed management, nursing and psychiatry teams can cause duplicate or missed assignments.
Delays in clinical authorization increase duration of unsafe observation and escalate patient risk.
Security or transport constraints can block a planned transfer even when room and clinical approval exist.
